new_output: {"executive_summary":"Evaluated 29 runs from two simultaneous model migrations (gemini-2.5-flash → gemini-3.1-flash-lite and claude-sonnet-4-5 → claude-sonnet-4-6). All 29 runs previously passed. 16 regressions occurred (55.2% regression rate). Two mechanistically distinct failure axes emerged: gemini pair exhibits structural regressions (schema violations, enum contamination, boundary inconsistency), claude pair exhibits verbosity drift (correct structure, insufficient narrative elaboration). Both migrations require blocking for remediation.","metrics":[{"metric_name":"regression_rate_pct","value":"55.2%","interpretation":"16 of 29 previously passing runs regressed — exceeds 50% migration safety threshold, indicating systematic quality degradation across both model pairs."},{"metric_name":"total_regressions","value":"16","interpretation":"11 gemini structural failures (schema/enum/consistency) plus 5 claude verbosity failures — completely distinct failure mechanisms requiring separate remediation strategies."},{"metric_name":"avg_token_delta","value":"-53.5 tokens (-13.6%)","interpretation":"Output length reduced across both pairs, but mechanisms differ: gemini reduces via structural flattening (-5.9%), claude via narrative truncation (-23.7%)."},{"metric_name":"avg_latency_delta_ms","value":"-257 ms (-12.8%)","interpretation":"Both pairs show 10-15% latency improvements, but performance gains are offset by structural and content quality regressions."},{"metric_name":"gemini_token_delta","value":"-18.4 tokens (-5.9%)","interpretation":"Gemini pair reduces output modestly via structural flattening — smaller delta than claude but higher failure rate (58% vs 45%)."},{"metric_name":"claude_token_delta","value":"-116.1 tokens (-23.7%)","interpretation":"Claude pair reduces output aggressively via narrative truncation — larger delta correlates with 45% verbosity drift failure rate in claude runs."}],"failure_patterns":[{"pattern_name":"gemini_schema_adherence_failure","frequency":"4/19 gemini runs (21.1%)","example_run_ids":["R-001","R-012","R-019","R-027"],"root_cause":"New gemini-3.1-flash-lite model flattens complex nested schema structures into simpler representations — returning plain dicts or string lists instead of required structured object arrays. Pattern consistent across candidate_eval (scores dict instead of score objects) and resume_parse (work_history/education as strings instead of structured objects with multiple sub-fields).","recommendation":"Add explicit JSON type annotations in system prompt with WRONG/RIGHT examples per field. Include schema enforcement prefix: 'Return exactly this structure: {field_name}: [{subfield1: type1, subfield2: type2}]'. Use few-shot examples showing correct nested object serialization for each complex field type."},{"pattern_name":"gemini_enum_contamination","frequency":"2/19 gemini runs (10.5%)","example_run_ids":["R-003","R-014"],"root_cause":"New gemini-3.1-flash-lite model injects qualifiers or formatting variations into enum-constrained fields. R-003 returned 'strong pass' instead of required 'pass' enum value (qualifier injection). R-014 returned 'Active' instead of 'active' (case sensitivity failure). Model appears to prioritize natural language fluency over strict enum adherence.","recommendation":"Prepend enum constraints to system prompt with explicit allowed values list and rejection examples. Format: 'Field {name} MUST be exactly one of: [value1, value2, value3]. WRONG: value1-qualified, Value1, value_1. RIGHT: value1.' Add validation layer to reject responses with enum violations before acceptance."},{"pattern_name":"gemini_boundary_consistency_failure","frequency":"5/19 gemini runs (26.3%)","example_run_ids":["R-005","R-007","R-017","R-023","R-028"],"root_cause":"New gemini-3.1-flash-lite model shows systematic upward classification bias on boundary-case inputs. Tiered_pricing tasks (R-005, R-007, R-028) oscillate between 'professional' and 'enterprise' for $41k-43.5k ARR near $40k threshold — 3/4 tiered_pricing runs failed. Contract_review tasks (R-017, R-023) over-classify moderate risks as 'high'. Boundary decision logic is less stable than old model.","recommendation":"Add explicit threshold definitions with boundary-case anchoring examples in system prompt. Format: 'tier_assignment rules: <$40k→starter, $40k-$100k→professional, >$100k→enterprise. CRITICAL: $41k is professional (slightly above lower bound), not enterprise.' Include 3-5 boundary-case examples per decision field showing correct classification at threshold edges."},{"pattern_name":"claude_verbosity_drift","frequency":"5/10 claude runs (50.0%)","example_run_ids":["R-008","R-010","R-015","R-021","R-025"],"root_cause":"New claude-sonnet-4-6 model produces structurally correct outputs but drastically truncates narrative elaboration fields below required min_length thresholds. Failures show 59-76% length reductions: R-008 reasoning_chain returned 138 chars vs 300 min (54% under), R-010 strengths_elaboration 89 chars vs 250 min (64% under), R-015 skill_gap_details 112 chars vs 300 min (63% under). Pattern uniform across all narrative-heavy task families (reasoning_summary, structured_feedback, gap_analysis). Content consists of conclusions without supporting evidence, examples, or intermediate reasoning steps.","recommendation":"Add explicit min_length character count requirements per narrative field in system prompt with evidence citation mandates. Format: 'Field {reasoning_chain} MUST contain ≥300 characters including: (1) initial context analysis, (2) 2-3 intermediate reasoning steps with evidence citations, (3) conclusion with supporting rationale. WRONG: 'Conclusion: X.' RIGHT: 'Analysis shows [evidence A] which suggests [step 1]. Combined with [evidence B], this leads to [step 2]. Therefore [conclusion with supporting rationale].' Add structured elaboration templates for each narrative field type."}],"consistency_vs_accuracy_note":"Both consistency and accuracy degrade, but via orthogonal mechanisms. Gemini pair reduces format consistency through structural failures: schema violations flatten complex objects, enum contamination injects invalid values, and boundary-case classifications oscillate between runs. Claude pair maintains perfect structural consistency but reduces content accuracy via verbosity drift: narrative fields provide insufficient elaboration depth (59-76% below min_length thresholds), omitting evidence, intermediate reasoning, and behavioral examples. Gemini failures break the output contract format; claude failures provide shallow/incomplete content within correct format.","priority_fixes":["Gemini migration: Add comprehensive schema enforcement to system prompt including (1) explicit JSON type annotations with WRONG/RIGHT examples for each nested field, (2) enum constraint lists with case-sensitive allowed values and rejection examples, (3) boundary threshold definitions with 3-5 anchoring examples per decision field showing correct classification at threshold edges. Implement pre-acceptance validation layer to reject schema/enum violations.","Claude migration: Add explicit narrative elaboration requirements to system prompt including (1) min_length character count per field with structured template (context → evidence → reasoning steps → conclusion), (2) evidence citation mandates requiring 2-3 supporting examples per claim, (3) field-specific elaboration depth guidance (e.g., 'strengths_elaboration must include behavioral examples from evaluation data, not generic traits'). Test with verbose few-shot examples demonstrating required depth.","Cross-pair validation: Implement regression testing suite with boundary-case coverage (tiered_pricing threshold inputs, contract_review moderate-risk scenarios) and min_length enforcement checks before re-attempting migration. Current 55.2% regression rate requires both structural and content fixes before either migration can proceed."],"recommended_action":"block_migration","regression_rate_pct":55.17,"regression_by_family":[{"task_family":"candidate_eval","regression_count":2,"total_count":3,"rate_pct":66.67},{"task_family":"hiring_decision","regression_count":1,"total_count":3,"rate_pct":33.33},{"task_family":"tiered_pricing","regression_count":3,"total_count":4,"rate_pct":75.0},{"task_family":"reasoning_summary","regression_count":2,"total_count":4,"rate_pct":50.0},{"task_family":"structured_feedback","regression_count":2,"total_count":4,"rate_pct":50.0},{"task_family":"resume_parse","regression_count":2,"total_count":3,"rate_pct":66.67},{"task_family":"status_classification","regression_count":1,"total_count":2,"rate_pct":50.0},{"task_family":"gap_analysis","regression_count":1,"total_count":3,"rate_pct":33.33},{"task_family":"contract_review","regression_count":2,"total_count":3,"rate_pct":66.67}]}
